notes: ParseRoleFile/ParseRoleBytes/validateRole are the single shared parser+validator for both corpora; phase is directory-inferred (rolecorpus.go). Review-side dedup lives in cluster.go/overlap.go; staleness is phase-parameterized via ComputeRolesHash(phase).
outputContractInstruction stamps role/location/class/severity into every review prompt and adds status only for PhaseAuditors; role files cannot redefine schema (rolefile.go allowedRoleKeys).
buildReviewPrompts/generateCorpusReviewPrompt produce prompt strings emitted as JSON; no sub-agent is spawned anywhere. Same for audit (audit.go).
No new Workflow/config knob added in v0.25.0; TestNonGoals_NoNewConfigWorkflowField guards it. Role schema is exactly the 5 fields (model/role.go).
ResolveActiveCorpus falls back to the embedded DefaultCorpus when no user role files exist, so a project with zero files runs the curated corpus.
validateRole enforces id==stem, kebab-case regex, and reserved regression rejection; model.Role.ID json key is id.
title required (rejected when blank after trim); model.Role.Title json:"title".
instructions required (rejected when blank after trim); model.Role.Instructions json:"instructions".
Focus []string json:"focus,omitempty"; appended as review focus questions (review.go:883) and audit Role Rules (audit_roles.go:126).
model.Role.Domains optional; roleAppliesToDomain treats empty/absent domains as every domain, otherwise membership-filtered.
defaultCorpusOrder reviewers: software implementer/tester/architect, prose coherence/soundness; corpus JSON files present for all.
defaultCorpusOrder auditors: software spec-coverage/security/maintainability-conventions, prose spec-coverage/soundness; all 10 corpus files exist.
Domain persona-swap is absorbed into domain-selected embedded corpora (ResolveActiveCorpus + defaultCorpusOrder); no Go persona switch remains in review.go.
Project-wide concerns become committed role files under .tp/reviewers|auditors loaded once via LoadRoleCorpus.
Spec-local concerns handled by tp.review_roles/tp.audit_roles focus overrides layered additively at emission (ResolveOverrideFocus).
tp emits prompts (JSON result) and never spawns agents; TestNonGoals_EmitsPromptsNoRuntime asserts prompt emission without runtime.
Any top-level key outside {id,title,instructions,focus,domains} is a validation error, so no user-defined finding schema/taxonomy is possible; contract stays tp's.
OverlapReport only reports per-role unique/shared+trim_candidate; buildReviewPrompts always emits the full active panel (no auto-taper).
No new .tp/config.json workflow field; TestNonGoals_NoNewConfigWorkflowField confirms the Workflow struct is unchanged by v0.25.0.
Role has exactly the 5 spec fields; rolefile.go allowedRoleKeys rejects any extra key. No speculative fields.
Frontmatter overrides only extend focus of an existing active role; an override id matching no active role is ignored with a warning (never defines a new role).
Discovery anchors at DiscoverTPDir (git-boundary .tp/) — single project, no cross-project/global corpus (same anchor in rolehash.go:25).
countAuditFindings does plain row counting (status != PASS); no clustering. Clustering (cluster.go/review_merge.go) applies to review findings only.
One-distinct-failure-lens guidance present, tied to overlap_report trim candidates.
Adversarial-framing guidance present (refute/find where it breaks vs check-if-fine).
Evidence-demand guidance present: every finding needs a location and a why to make dedup/audit meaningful.
Scope-boundaries guidance present: state what the role does NOT cover to keep lenses disjoint.
Output-contract-adherence guidance present; enforced in code by rolefile.go rejecting non-schema keys.
model.Role with required id/title/instructions and optional focus/domains string arrays; JSON key is focus not lens. Struct tests exist (frontmatter/defaultcorpus tests parse it).
Single shared ParseRoleFile/ParseRoleBytes/validateRole enforces id==stem, kebab-case, reserved regression, unknown-key rejection, and domains-array. Used by both corpora.
LoadRoleCorpus reads .tp/<phase>/*.json at the git-boundary anchor; populated iff >=1 .json; populated replaces embedded, absent/empty returns populated=false; non-JSON ignored.

Synthesized prompt-design guidance (adversarial framing, evidence demand, scope boundaries, one-lens-per-role) is recorded in SKILL §Role authoring guidance and embodied in the embedded corpus role prompts; research process itself is not a code artifact but its output is observable.
Embedded corpus //go:embed corpus with all 10 role files matching the §5.1 table for both domains; DefaultCorpus parses/validates each. eject_neutral_test asserts byte-identical emission.

ResolveActiveCorpus selects embedded corpus by domain when no files, filters by role.Domains, unknown-domain->software with warning, empty-panel fallback to full domain-selected embedded corpus; prose two-reviewer default via defaultCorpusOrder.

ComputeRolesHash: sha256 over user files sorted by repo-relative slash path, hashing path+CRLF-normalized content; returns builtin sentinel with no files; regression role is never a file so never included.

buildReviewPrompts emits one generateCorpusReviewPrompt per domain-filtered reviewer, appends built-in regression (never a corpus file) when a diff/fixed finding exists; full panel each counted round, no auto-drop. Prompts emitted, not executed.

generateRoleAuditPrompts emits one prompt per active auditor role from the domain-filtered corpus (audit.go:181-202), Role Rules drawn from corpus role.Focus instead of a hardcoded map; symmetric full-panel emission.

outputContractInstruction stamps role/location/class/severity for review and adds status for PhaseAuditors; audit_roles.go:159-160 appends it with PhaseAuditors. location described as first §-token anchor. emission_contract_test covers both phases.
LocationKey uses regexp §\d+(\.\d+)* FindString for the first token (so §8 vs §8.2 stay distinct); no-token location returns the trimmed string verbatim.
ClusterFindings groups by (LocationKey, trimmed class); an empty-after-trim class forms its own singleton so an empty key never collapses unrelated findings.

Representative via lessRepresentative (severity rank, then role, then finding); Attribution returns sorted distinct roles excluding regression and blank, count=cardinality, so a regression-only cluster is found_by 0 with empty roles.

clusterMergeFindings (split out of runReviewMerge) clusters by (location key,class) via engine.ClusterFindings and emits representatives with found_by/found_by_roles, replacing literal-text dedup. Audit --record path (countAuditFindings) untouched. Behavior-preserving refactor verified.

OverlapReport computes per-role unique(sole-contributor) and shared(found_by>=2), flags unique==0 && shared>=1 as trim candidate, excludes regression/blank, no corpus-global ratio. Surfaced in --merge (review_merge.go:53), --report (review_report.go:107-111), --status (review_status.go:61).

computeMechanizeCandidates keeps cross-round class-frequency logic unchanged; no found_by threshold constant added (found_by appears only in cluster/overlap/merge code, never in report mechanize logic).

review --record stores reviewer RolesHash on the round entry (like spec_hash); audit_record.go:88-113 stores auditor hash on AuditRounds. Review/audit sequences independent (separate ReviewRounds/AuditRounds). roles_hash_record_test covers it.

review --status computes current corpus hash and reports roles_stale via engine.RolesStale (read-only); audit --status same (audit_record.go:166-174). RolesStale treats empty stored hash (pre-v0.25.0) as matching (reviewstate.go:183-192).
ParseFrontmatterBytes parses tp.review_roles/tp.audit_roles into Frontmatter.ReviewRoles/AuditRoles; parseRoleOverrides permits only focus (string array) and warns+ignores any other key.
ResolveOverrideFocus appends override focus after corpus focus (project-first), warns on unknown id, and never receives the regression role (appended separately), so regression accepts no overrides.

TranslateLegacyLens auto-translates lens.all to every active review role and lens.<id> to that role (unknown id warned) with a deprecation warning; when review_roles/audit_roles present the new form wins and lens is ignored (no three-way merge). Only invoked for review when no new overrides (overrides.go:29-39).

Prompts are corpus-driven (generateCorpusReviewPrompt from role fields); no `domain ==` branch or persona swap in review.go. domain now only selects/filters the corpus (corpusdomain.go). domain_lens_test covers it.

lintRoleCorpusOrAbort validates both phases via LoadRoleCorpus and exits 3 with a repair-or-delete hint; review aborts on a bad reviewer (review.go:770-774), audit on a bad auditor (audit.go:185-190) preserving phase independence.

runEjectRoles writes DefaultCorpusFiles into .tp/reviewers|auditors; unknown --domain is ExitUsage(2) listing known domains; refuses overwrite unless --force which writes regardless of validity. Flags wired in newInitCmd:34-36.
DiscoverTaskFile precedence is explicit>TP_FILE>.tp/local.json active>auto-detect with no .tp-active read; use.go resolvedActiveSource (57-77) mirrors this. Legacy marker no longer in the chain.
Root .gitignore no longer lists .tp-active; gitignore_test.go:20 asserts .tp-active must not be gitignored after v0.25.0.
TestEject_EmissionNeutral asserts byte-identical prompts/panel; TestEject_FlipsHashAndStalesOnce asserts the phase hash flips builtin->file hash and marks convergence stale once.
Five tests: EmitsPromptsNoRuntime, NoAutoTaper, NoNewRoleFromFrontmatter, NoAuditClustering, NoNewConfigWorkflowField cover non-goals 1-8.
TestMigration_NoRoleFilesIdenticalPanel, PreV0250RoundCarriesForward, and TPActiveNeedsNoAction cover the three §13 migration paths.

§Role authoring guidance covers the 5 principles, worked example role sets for software and prose, and a catalog of legal/contract, product/PRD, data-schema, academic domains; notes ejected defaults are worked examples.
README has zero .tp-active refs; SKILL.md:231, REFERENCE.md:152, CLAUDE.md:146 mention it only as removed-in-v0.25.0 history and describe .tp/local.json as the sole active pointer.
README documents tp init --eject-roles with --domain/--force plus review_roles/audit_roles overrides, per-role overlap, and roles_stale (8 keyword hits across the role-corpus section).
REFERENCE documents the eject command+flags, role-corpus schema, frontmatter overrides, overlap fields, roles_stale, and the exit-3 malformed-role abort (21 keyword hits).
SKILL documents tp init --eject-roles (157), the role corpus, corpus-driven emission, per-role overlap_report/roles_stale, and role validation in lint/review/audit.

CLAUDE.md self-dev rules describe the v0.25.0 role corpus, eject workflow, removed .tp-active, and that tp's repo stays on the embedded corpus while still dogfooding emission/dedup/staleness (no repo migration).

No file writes; git invoked via exec.Command("git", args...) with base/tag passed as discrete argv elements (no shell, no injection); affected files validated with os.Stat and directory-rejected before use; graceful os.ReadFile fallbacks are documented (optional task/findings files).

Round file written 0o600 under engine.WithReviewStateLock (round-file-first, index-second); write/hash/state errors checked and exit; rolesHash,_ := ComputeRolesHash intentionally ignores an informational hash error (empty hash treated as matching, no security impact); JSON parsed with checked errors.

No writes or locks; parseSpecFile uses defer Close and checks scanner.Err; task-file reads degrade gracefully (optional file); paths via filepath.Join; input validated (allowlist phases, regex rules). No security surface.

Only write is snapshot at L587 with 0o600, path via filepath.Join; extensive input validation (perspective allowlist L303, os.Stat on docs/test/diff/affected, dir-rejection L302-406); no lock acquired here so no unpaired-release risk; snapshot bytes are idempotent spec copies. buildReviewPrompts refactor preserves corpusErr->exit path; no new defect.

loadMergeFindings validates files exist, opens with per-iteration Close, skips invalid/incomplete rows, aborts on empty; output written 0o600 (L59) to user-specified path (single-writer, not shared state, no lock needed). Split refactor behavior-preserving; no new defect.

Round file written 0o600 under WithReviewStateLock; all state/read/hash errors checked and exit; %v->%w refactor at L145 correctly wraps the parse error for errors chain while L44/L59 %v are terminal output strings (correct). rolesHash,_ ignores informational hash error only (documented fallback). No new defect.

Read-only: os.Stat validates spec/diffFrom/findings before use; no writes, no locks. String concat builds label/prompt text (L43,L98), not file paths or queries. mechChecks,_ discards the allPass bool (not an error).

Read-only compute+print; parseNDJSONFile uses defer Close and returns scanner.Err; invalid JSON lines skipped by design; paths via filepath.Join; fmt.Fprintln to stdout with _,_ = is standard. No writes or locks.

No writes, no locks; spec validated via os.Stat; mechanical checks run user-configured commands validated by ValidateChecks (own config, not external injection). rolesHash,_ := ComputeRolesHash ignores an informational hash error only (empty hash = matching per 9.4).

setActiveFile validates target is inside project root and rejects path traversal (rel HasPrefix "..", L155); local.json read+write under engine.WithFileLock (paired release via helper defer); WriteLocalConfig writes 0o600; MkdirAll 0o755 for dir; os.Remove error checked in clearActiveFile.

Static role-definition data file (id/title/instructions/focus); no code, locks, error handling, paths, or file writes. Validated by ParseRoleBytes at load; well-formed JSON matching the security role's focus checklist.

defaultCorpusRaw builds path by string concat (L79), but domain/phase are allowlist-validated against defaultCorpusOrder before reach and id comes from the internal authored ids slice; target is the read-only embed.FS (defaultCorpusFS) which cannot be escaped by traversal. No external-input path injection; all read errors wrapped and returned. No writes/locks.

Pure parser; no writes or locks. Missing/unreadable file degrades to documented defaults (L42). Every YAML value type-asserted with warnings before use; unknown/malformed keys ignored safely. No path/query concat from input.
Pure in-memory translation function; no file I/O, no locks, no writes, no error returns. Deterministic map/slice manipulation over already-parsed frontmatter. No security surface.

WithReviewStateLock delegates to WithFileLock (paired Unlock+Remove via defer on all paths); SaveReviewState writes state.json 0o600; MkdirAll 0o755 for dir; corrupt/unparseable state surfaces StateCorruptError (never silently rebuilt). Paths via filepath.Join; LoadRoundRows/hasStateArtifacts skip-on-error is documented.

Strong input validation before use: strict allowedRoleKeys rejection, id must equal filename stem, kebab-case regex, reserved-id guard, required-field checks; all errors wrapped with %w. Read-only (os.ReadFile), no writes or locks; RoleStem via filepath.Base/Ext, no concat.

Only exported symbol ChecklistItem has a doc comment (L34-35). runAudit is 107 lines - a cohesive linear command handler within the ~110 tolerance; all other funcs <80. Errors surfaced terminally via output.Error+os.Exit (not propagated), so %w N/A. camelCase names, no TODO/FIXME.

All funcs <80 (runAuditRecord 65, recordAuditRoundEntry 27, countAuditFindings 19, runAuditStatus 49). Propagated JSON parse error wrapped with %w (L128). No exported symbols, camelCase naming, no TODO/FIXME.
All 11 funcs <50 lines (largest buildRolePrompt 49). No exported symbols; errors surfaced terminally via output.Error (no propagation, %w N/A). Descriptive camelCase names, no TODO/FIXME.
All funcs <60 (newInitCmd 14, runInit 57, runEjectRoles 47). No exported symbols, no propagated fmt.Errorf, camelCase naming, no TODO/FIXME.
All funcs <80 (runLint 76, checkAffectedFilesScope 72, checkTaskFileQuality 63). Structs lintResult/lintFrontmatter are unexported. Errors terminal via output.Error, camelCase naming, no TODO/FIXME.

Round-1 fix (c) verified: buildReviewPrompts (L765-845, 81 lines, doc-commented) was correctly extracted. But runReview remains 427 lines - ~4x the ~110 command-handler tolerance. Its three perspective branches (code-audit L447-465, documentation L467-500, testing L502-535) each independently build+return a reviewResult and are readily extractable. buildFindingsSummary 117 and newReviewCmd 127 (cobra constructor) are borderline-acceptable. No exported-symbol/naming/error-wrap/TODO issues.

Round-1 fix (b) verified: runReviewMerge now 69 lines, split into loadMergeFindings (43, doc-commented L85-88) and clusterMergeFindings (34, doc-commented L133-135); all helpers <80 and documented. Terminal errors via output.Error, camelCase naming, no TODO/FIXME.
All 3 funcs tiny (<17 lines). No exported symbols, no propagated errors, descriptive camelCase names, no TODO/FIXME.

Round-1 fix (a) verified: parseRecordRows wraps the JSON parse error with %w (L145, was %v). runReviewRecord 111 lines - a cohesive linear handler at the ~110 tolerance edge; other funcs <30. Display-only %v in output.Error terminal paths (L44,59) is not propagation. No exported symbols, camelCase naming, no TODO/FIXME.
runReviewRegression 102 lines (within ~110 handler tolerance); collectFixedFindings 20, buildRegressionPrompt 30. No exported symbols, no propagated fmt.Errorf, camelCase naming, no TODO/FIXME.
